extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won from VAN POPPEL Danny's leadout after REYNDERS Jens survived two breakaways and was caught on the final Mammolshain lap.
tactical_patterns: REYNDERS Jens entered both breakaways including being dropped then rechasing, last caught showing Sport Vlaanderen's aggressive fatigue tactics.
ZIMMERMANN Georg attempted mid-race detachment to pressure rival sprint teams rather than conserving for leadout duties.
WRIGHT Fred opened final sprint for GIRMAY Biniam but Intermarché lacked second leadout man allowing VAN POPPEL full train.
KRISTOFF Alexander positioned in GIRMAY Biniam's wheel tactically rather than using own leadout for fifth win attempt.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: returned to top sprinter form after difficult season, finished cleanly from VAN POPPEL leadout
rider_signals.REYNDERS Jens: positive: extraordinary resilience entering both breakaways, rechasing after dropped, forcing peloton work until final Mammolshain lap
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: delivered textbook final leadout allowing celebration before line, key upgrade to BENNETT's sprint train
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: fifth win attempt showed tactical awareness wheel-surfing GIRMAY rather than forcing own team tempo
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: neutral: moved up quickly on return from Eritrea but WRIGHT Fred solo leadout insufficient against VAN POPPEL train
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: second place without visible leadout shows raw speed remains despite team deficit
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: neutral: fourth despite Bahrain's work suggests positioning errors in technical Frankfurt finale
rider_signals.RUTSCH Jonas: neutral: strong in second breakaway but EF lacked numerical advantage to sustain effort into finale
rider_signals.ZIMMERMANN Georg: neutral: mid-race aggression by Bora domestique unusual but failed to fracture peloton before regrouping
